# Research Rubric

Use this reference when filling `02-competitor-analysis.md`.

## Benchmark Account Criteria

A benchmark account is useful only if it is:

- in the same or adjacent vertical
- actively posting in the last 30 days
- visibly getting engagement, not just followers
- close enough in business model to reveal transferable patterns

Prefer 3-5 benchmark accounts per client. Avoid celebrity accounts unless the client is already a large creator or brand.

## Capture Schema

For each benchmark account, capture:

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| Handle / name | Copy as displayed |
| Approx. follower level | Exact number if visible, otherwise band |
| Persona type | Expert, founder, diary, curated brand |
| Bio structure | Keywords, proof, CTA style |
| Posting cadence | Notes per week and common posting windows |
| Top content buckets | 3-5 recurring themes |
| Strongest note pattern | What repeatedly performs |

For each benchmark note, capture:

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| Note title | Exact or near-exact wording |
| Content type | How-to, checklist, before/after, review, diary, myth-busting |
| Cover style | Text-led, face-led, product-led, collage, proof-led |
| Visible metrics | Likes, collects, comments, views if visible |
| Hook angle | Problem, aspiration, warning, speed, social proof |
| Search keywords | Words that appear in title/body/tags |

## Keyword Harvesting

Build three buckets:

- Core keywords: the main searched phrases in the vertical
- Long-tail keywords: intent-rich phrases with narrower competition
- Trigger keywords: seasonal or topical terms that can be attached to a note

Prefer keywords users type naturally. Avoid stuffing the same term into every field.

## Minimum Research Output

Do not call research complete until all of these are true:

- 3+ benchmark accounts recorded
- 15+ benchmark notes recorded
- 3-5 core keywords chosen
- 10+ long-tail keywords mapped
- 2-3 repeatable content patterns identified

## Browser Fallback

If live browsing is unavailable, require one or more of:

- exported note URLs
- screenshots of search results and note pages
- copied benchmark note lists
- previous studio research artifacts

Record the fallback source in `02-competitor-analysis.md` so later operators know whether the research was live or indirect.
